brief_document.title: End-to-end engine integration tests over InMemoryStore
brief_document.purpose: Prove the engine works end to end against InMemoryStore: start records the right event and registers a discoverable handle, cancel tears down activity children and records the cancellation, and completion yields the recorded result. These are the cross-cutting tests that exercise the whole lifecycle through the public Engine API.
brief_document.task: Build integration tests in crates/aion/tests that drive a real Engine over InMemoryStore using a committed minimal workflow fixture (R0) that dispatches an in-VM activity. Assert the start/register/list path, the cancel/teardown/append path, and the complete/result path. Tests run a real runtime at runtime — never gated out or #[ignore]'d.

requirements.title: Committed minimal workflow beam fixture
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a committed, minimal compiled workflow fixture the integration tests load through the runtime, with its source and a documented regeneration step, so the tests have a real beam to execute.
acceptance: crates/aion/tests/fixtures/ holds a minimal workflow fixture: its source (a tiny hand-written .erl or .gleam module exposing an entry function that completes with a known result and one that dispatches an in-VM activity) AND its compiled .beam, both committed.

crates/aion/tests/fixtures/README.md documents the exact regeneration command (e.g. erlc / gleam build) and states the fixture is checked in so the test suite needs no Gleam/Erlang toolchain at test time.
A test loads the fixture .beam through the RuntimeHandle and asserts the entry module/function resolve, establishing the fixture is valid before the lifecycle tests use it.
The fixture is deliberately tiny (no external deps) so it stays maintainable and its bytes are reviewable.

requirements.title: Start, append, register, and list
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an integration test that builds an Engine over InMemoryStore, starts a test workflow, and asserts WorkflowStarted is appended, the handle is registered, and list_workflows reports it.
acceptance: tests/engine_lifecycle.rs builds an Engine over a fresh InMemoryStore with one loaded test workflow.
After start_workflow, the store history's first event is WorkflowStarted with the workflow type and input.
The registry contains a Running handle for the new (WorkflowId, RunId) and list_workflows returns a matching WorkflowSummary.
The test runs under cargo test (real runtime), not gated out or ignored.

requirements.title: Cancel tears down activity child and appends cancellation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an integration test that starts a workflow which dispatches an in-VM activity child, cancels the workflow, and asserts the activity child is no longer alive and WorkflowCancelled is appended.
acceptance: The test workflow dispatches an in-VM activity (AE-007) as a linked child that stays alive long enough to observe.
After cancel, the activity child process is no longer alive (link propagation) and the store history ends with WorkflowCancelled carrying the reason.
The registry no longer holds the handle after cancel.
The test is a real runtime test, not gated out or ignored.

requirements.title: Completion yields the recorded result
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an integration test that starts a workflow which completes and asserts result() returns the recorded result Payload and the final history event is WorkflowCompleted.
acceptance: The test workflow runs to completion returning a known value.
result() returns the recorded result Payload equal to the workflow's output.
The store history's final event is WorkflowCompleted carrying that Payload, and list_workflows reports the workflow as Completed.
The test is a real runtime test, not gated out or ignored.

boundaries: SHALL NOT require a database — all tests use InMemoryStore (CO13).
SHALL NOT use #[ignore] or feature-gate the tests out; any runtime requirement is satisfied by the embedded beamr runtime the test builds.
SHALL NOT test AT/AD mechanics (signal routing, replay, durable timers) beyond the AE lifecycle surface — those are exercised in AT/AD.
verification: cargo test -p aion passes, including the integration tests.
Tests run a real embedded runtime against InMemoryStore with no #[ignore] and no database.

checklist.text: An integration test builds an Engine over InMemoryStore, starts a test workflow, and asserts WorkflowStarted is appended and the handle is registered and reported by list_workflows.
An integration test asserts cancelling a workflow kills its linked activity child and appends WorkflowCancelled, and that a completed workflow's result() returns the recorded Payload.

stories.text: As an engine operator, I want to build an Engine from a store, loaded workflows, and my NIFs with one builder so that embedding durable workflows in my Rust app is a single, clear assembly step.
As a workflow caller, I want to cancel a running workflow and have its in-flight activities torn down automatically so that cancellation leaves no orphaned work.
As a workflow caller, I want to await a workflow's result and receive either its output payload or its terminal error so that I can react to the outcome.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a runtime gate it at runtime, never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion depends only on aion-core, aion-store, aion-package, and beamr among workspace crates — no aion-server, no store backend crate, no networking crate. Structural; must hold.
Transport-agnostic: no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket dependency or code anywhere in the crate.
Only the runtime module imports beamr; every other module uses the RuntimeHandle (per D1).
All library errors are thiserror EngineError variants; no anyhow in this library crate. No .unwrap()/.expect() outside tests.
Mutex/RwLock poison handled explicitly and mapped to a typed EngineError; no .unwrap() on a lock guard.
WorkflowStatus is read from aion-core's event projection; the registry's cached status is never the source of truth (per aion-core CO7).
aion hardcodes no scheduler thread count, no timeout, and no retry default; configurable values come from the builder or are deferred to beamr's own default.
Module namespacing uses aion-package's transform unchanged; the engine never re-derives namespaced names (per D8).
Engine tests run against InMemoryStore; no test requires a database (per aion-core D8).

resolved_context.intention: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows, runs each as a BEAM process on an embedded beamr runtime, supervises them through a three-level tree, dispatches their activities as linked child processes, and cancels or reports on them by ID. Starting a workflow is spawning a process; cancelling it is killing that process, which through links kills its activity children too; an activity crash propagates an exit signal the trapping workflow process receives as a message. The crate is transport-agnostic — no HTTP/gRPC/WebSocket — keeping the embedded path free of network dependencies. This cluster owns lifecycle, process management, supervision, and module loading; it defers durability/replay (AD) and time/signals/queries/children/concurrency (AT) to their owners, surfacing their entry points on Engine and stating the seams explicitly.

provenance.quote: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows,
